messages.content:
You are an expert Python software engineer specialising in
dependency management and Python version compatibility. You have
deep knowledge of the Python ecosystem including packaging tools
(pip, poetry, hatch, setuptools), common libraries, and the
breaking changes introduced across Python versions 3.10 through
3.13. When migrating a project you methodically read the project
structure before making changes, understand root causes rather
than patching symptoms, and verify your fixes by running the
actual test suite. Always create and work inside a fresh virtual
environment. Never install packages into the system Python.

Please solve this issue: This Python repository was developed for an older Python version and
currently fails under Python 3.13.

The error output from attempting to run it on Python 3.13 is shown
below. Read the repository carefully before attempting any fix.

=== Python 3.13 Error Output ===
**Docker image:** `Dockerfile.py313`
**Python version:** 3.13
**Result:** Build fails at install step — `astropy` cannot be built

### Error — `setuptools.dep_util` removed

```
File "astropy/wcs/setup_package.py", line 13, in <module>
    from setuptools.dep_util import newer_group
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'setuptools.dep_util'
```

**Root cause:** `astropy >=5.0,<6` (required by poliastro) uses `setuptools.dep_util` in its build system (`astropy/wcs/setup_package.py`). The `dep_util` submodule was removed from setuptools in version 58.0 (2021). On Python 3.13, pip's build isolation environment pulls in a modern setuptools (>=58), causing astropy's wheel build to fail before any code can run.

**Minimal fix:** Upgrade `astropy` to `>=6.0` which rewrites its build system to not use `setuptools.dep_util`, making it compatible with modern setuptools and Python 3.13.

---
=== End of Error Output ===

Your goal is to make this repository fully functional under Python 3.13.

You must produce two things:

1. Any changes to source files, pyproject.toml, setup.cfg, setup.py,
   or other project files needed to make the project compatible with
   Python 3.13

2. A file called Dockerfile.py313 in the repository root that:
   - Uses python:3.13-slim as the base image
   - Installs the project and all its dependencies
   - Runs the full test suite using RUN (not CMD), so that test
     failures cause the docker build itself to fail
   - Exits 0 if and only if all tests pass

   Example structure:
   FROM python:3.13-slim
   WORKDIR /app
   COPY . /app
   RUN pip install ...
   RUN python -m pytest tests/ -q

Before submitting, verify your Dockerfile works by running:
    docker build -f Dockerfile.py313 -t swe_verify_$(date +%s) .
This must exit 0 before you submit. If it fails, read the error,
fix the problem, and rebuild until it passes.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove, skip, or weaken existing tests. You may update test
  code only to adapt to genuine API or behaviour changes in dependencies
- Your Dockerfile must work from a clean python:3.13-slim base with
  no pre-installed packages
- Always work inside fresh virtual environments, never the system Python
- In your Dockerfile, always use RUN to execute tests, never CMD

Funding = "https://opencollective.com/poliastro"

[project.optional-dependencies]
jupyter = ["notebook", "ipywidgets>=7.6"]
cesium = ["czml3 ~=0.5.3"]

    "sphinx-gallery<=0.7.0",  # Just to avoid the jupytext warning
    "sphinx_rtd_theme~=1.0.0",
    "sphinx-hoverxref==0.7b1",
    "sphinx-notfound-page",
    "sphinx-copybutton>0.2.9",
    "sphinx-github-role~=0.1.0",
]
test = [
    "coverage",
    "hypothesis",
    "import-linter[toml]",
    "mypy>=0.740",
    "pre-commit",
    "pytest>=3.2",
    "pytest-cov<2.6.0",  # https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-cov/issues/222
    "pytest-doctestplus>=0.8",  # https://github.com/astropy/pytest-doctestplus/issues/118
    "pytest-mpl",
    "pytest-mypy",
    "pytest-remotedata",
    "tox",
]

[tool.flit.sdist]
include = [
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa62.dat",
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa76.dat",
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa76_p.dat",
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa76_rho.dat",
    "setup.cfg"
]
exclude = [
    "docs/"
]

[tool.black]
line-length = 79

[tool.ruff]
select = [
    "E",  # pycodestyle, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#pycodestyle-e-w
#    "D",  # pydocstyle, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#pydocstyle-d
    "F",  # pyflakes, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#pyflakes-f
    "I"   # isort, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#isort-i
]

ignore = [
    "E501",  # Line too long. Ignoring this so "black" manages line length.
]

exclude = [
    ".git",
    ".tox",
    ".venv",
    "build",
    "dist",
]

[tool.ruff.isort]
combine-as-imports = true
force-sort-within-sections = true
known-first-party = ["poliastro"]

[tool.ruff.mccabe]
max-complexity = 18

[tool.mypy]
check_untyped_defs = true
strict_optional = false
ignore_missing_imports = true

[tool.coverage.paths]
source = ["src"]

[tool.coverage.run]
branch = true
source = ["src"]

[tool.coverage.report]
omit = ["contrib/*"]
show_missing = true

[tool.importlinter]
# Include Astropy to properly analyze external submodules,
# see https://github.com/seddonym/import-linter/issues/111
root_packages = ["poliastro", "astropy"]
include_external_packages = true

[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]
name = "poliastro.core does not import astropy.units"
type = "forbidden"
source_modules = ["poliastro.core"]
forbidden_modules = ["astropy.units"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
norecursedirs = [".git", ".tox", "dist", "build", ".venv"]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files ="test_*.py"
doctest_plus = false
addopts = ["--strict-markers"]
markers = ["slow", "remote_data", "filterwarnings", "mpl_image_compare"]
"
}

    "pytest-doctestplus>=0.8",  # https://github.com/astropy/pytest-doctestplus/issues/118
    "pytest-mpl",
    "pytest-mypy",
    "pytest-remotedata",
    "tox",
]

[tool.flit.sdist]
include = [
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa62.dat",
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa76.dat",
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa76_p.dat",
    "src/poliastro/atmosphere/data/coesa76_rho.dat",
    "setup.cfg"
]
exclude = [
    "docs/"
]

[tool.black]
line-length = 79

[tool.ruff]
select = [
    "E",  # pycodestyle, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#pycodestyle-e-w
#    "D",  # pydocstyle, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#pydocstyle-d
    "F",  # pyflakes, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#pyflakes-f
    "I"   # isort, see https://github.com/charliermarsh/ruff#isort-i
]

ignore = [
    "E501",  # Line too long. Ignoring this so "black" manages line length.
]

exclude = [
    ".git",
    ".tox",
    ".venv",
    "build",
    "dist",
]

[tool.ruff.isort]
combine-as-imports = true
force-sort-within-sections = true
known-first-party = ["poliastro"]

[tool.ruff.mccabe]
max-complexity = 18

[tool.mypy]
check_untyped_defs = true
strict_optional = false
ignore_missing_imports = true

[tool.coverage.paths]
source = ["src"]

[tool.coverage.run]
branch = true
source = ["src"]

[tool.coverage.report]
omit = ["contrib/*"]
show_missing = true

[tool.importlinter]
# Include Astropy to properly analyze external submodules,
# see https://github.com/seddonym/import-linter/issues/111
root_packages = ["poliastro", "astropy"]
include_external_packages = true

[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]
name = "poliastro.core does not import astropy.units"
type = "forbidden"
source_modules = ["poliastro.core"]
forbidden_modules = ["astropy.units"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
norecursedirs = [".git", ".tox", "dist", "build", ".venv"]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files ="test_*.py"
doctest_plus = false
addopts = ["--strict-markers"]
markers = ["slow", "remote_data", "filterwarnings", "mpl_image_compare"]

So we can't even import astropy 6.1.7 with numpy 2.4.6. The issue is that astropy 6.1.7 has a bug where `np.in1d` was deprecated in numpy 2.0 and removed in numpy 2.4. The `NUMPY_LT_2_0` guard doesn't help because it was designed for numpy versions below 2.0, but numpy 2.4 no longer has `in1d`.

So I need to either:
1. Upgrade to astropy 7.x (which probably fixed this)
2. Pin numpy to <2.4 (but that's not a good long-term solution)

Let me update the dependency to `"astropy >=7.0"` (no upper bound, or maybe `<8`).
